definition: The Treasury basis trade ($800B-$1T+ at 50-100x leverage) functions as synthetic QE by removing duration from private markets via repo financing, suppressing measured term premium by 15-25bp while amplifying systemic fragility at unprecedented scale.
evidence: All four debates accepted its existence and approximate scale
Japan 2003 VaR shock provides closest amplification mechanism analogue
open_questions: Would partial unwind (20-40%) release only ~5-10bp or more?
What triggers an unwind — margin calls, regulatory action, or voluntary position reduction?
Does the trade mask the true supply-demand imbalance enough to delay regime resolution?
